methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $800M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Elroy Air, a Series B autonomous drone company, is valued at $800M post-money on $116M in total raised. The drones and autonomous sector comprises eight private peers including Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Manna Aero, with no public comparables available.

Stage-progression analysis applies to this valuation. Using the $800M last post-money as a base and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups yields an implied valuation of $1.5B. Peer-median analysis is not applicable here; while four same-sector peers exist, insufficient narrow-filter matches (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) were identified to support a reliable median benchmark—fewer than the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical credibility.

The triangulation, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.5B. This suggests modest upside from the current $800M valuation. Key uncertainties include the composition and financials of the peer set; a more granular understanding of Zipline, Joby, and Nuro—in terms of revenue multiples, capital efficiency, and time-to-profitability—would strengthen conviction in this range. The absence of public comps limits external market validation. The range should be treated as preliminary pending acquisition of comparable financial detail.
